title: Carreg Goch (Wellington R1465) Bomber Memorial
memorial_type: Memorial cairn — highest-situated war memorial in UK (~2,500 ft)
narrative: In the early hours of 6 July 1942, an all-Canadian crew from 22 OTU at Wellesbourne Mountford were on a night cross-country navigation exercise. Fl/Sgt Kemp descended from 10,000 feet in heavy cloud to fix position visually. The Wellington struck the summit of Waun Rydd at approximately 2,500 feet. All five crew were killed and buried together at Hereford Cemetery in adjacent graves in Plot A. The memorial cairn on Waun Rydd, built in 1980 by Tredegar Comprehensive School students, is the highest-situated war memorial in the UK.

crew.name: John Beverley Kemp
crew.burial_location: Hereford Cemetery, Plot A (C of E), Grave 5619
crew.fate_detail: Killed when Wellington struck summit of Waun Rydd while descending in cloud. Buried at Hereford Cemetery.

cause_of_loss: Pilot descended from 10,000 ft in heavy cloud to fix position; Wellington struck summit of Waun Rydd
memorial_details: The highest-situated war memorial in the UK, at approximately 2,500 feet. Built in 1980 by Tredegar Comprehensive School. Accessible via 2-3 hour walk from Talybont-on-Usk or via Blaen-y-Glyn waterfalls trail (~8 km loop).

external_narratives.summary: Walking guide to the highest war memorial in the UK.
Official accident record for Wellington R1465.
Feature on Brecon Beacons crash sites.
key_details: Multiple crash sites in the area noted
